bead.title: bug: work command preserves legacy execute-loop routing surface
bead.description:
Review/evolution finding amended by passthrough requirement.
File: cli/cmd/work.go:8
Category: integration
Severity: high
Description: ddx work is currently an alias for ddx agent execute-loop and clones legacy routing behavior. This contradicts FEAT-010's layered model where ddx run is the invocation atom, ddx try wraps one bead attempt, and ddx work drains the queue and owns retry policy. However --harness/--provider/--model must remain available as passthrough-only operator constraints.
Suggested fix: Implement the FEAT-010 layers directly: ddx run -> Execute with requested power and optional passthrough envelope, ddx try <bead> -> run plus bead/worktree/evidence handling, ddx work -> queue iteration and retry-power policy. Remove DDx-owned routing semantics while preserving passthrough flags.
bead.acceptance: ddx work no longer clones execute-loop behavior; tests show work invokes try-layer behavior, owns retry-power policy between attempts, accepts harness/provider/model only as an opaque passthrough envelope, and does not validate or branch on passthrough string values.
